campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026, where they discussed staffing challenges and recruitment efforts for city positions. The council also heard public comments on various matters and announced the upcoming Boogie Festival happening that weekend.
keyTopics: City staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention strategies
Public hearing on government code section 3502 matters
Boogie Festival this weekend
Public comment period on council reports

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 6, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council approved an engineer's report for the current fiscal year and moved forward with a public hearing process for a new ordinance scheduled for final adoption on May 20th. The council also discussed whether Saratoga should become a charter city and reviewed preliminary estimates for potential new tax options and revenue sources.
keyTopics: Engineer's Report approval for FY 2026
New ordinance moving to adoption vote on May 20
Public hearing conducted during meeting
Evaluation of charter city status for Saratoga
Review of potential tax options and revenue estimates

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 2, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on June 2, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, confirm a new Youth Commissioner, and set up the November 2026 municipal election for three open seats. The council also approved a housing development map, authorized the town manager to sign several service agreements including a five-year contract with Accela Inc., and approved the budget.
keyTopics: November 3, 2026 general election scheduled for three Town Council seats
New Youth Commissioner appointment confirmed by selection committee
Final approval for Tract 10675 Boulevard housing development
Five-year software/services agreement with Accela Inc.
Budget authorization and multiple service contracts approved

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 3, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a planning meeting to organize two upcoming council sessions: one on June 9, 2026 (final agenda) and one on June 16, 2026 (draft agenda). The council reviewed which items to add, whether any items needed specific time slots, and approved free use of the Janet Gray Hayes Rotunda for an event on June 7, 2026.
keyTopics: June 9, 2026 council meeting final agenda organization
June 16, 2026 council meeting draft agenda organization
Janet Gray Hayes Rotunda free-use approval for June 7 event

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 4, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on June 4, 2026, with both in-person and online participation available. Residents could join virtually via Zoom or phone to watch proceedings and submit public comments.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom (ID: 812 5019 2776) or phone dial-in
Public comment procedures for virtual participants using 'raise hand' feature
Phone participants can unmute using *6 to speak during public comment periods

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 2, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held two back-to-back special meetings on June 2, 2026. The first was a closed session (private meeting) where sensitive matters were discussed, followed by a public special meeting with items designated as special orders of business.
keyTopics: Closed session held at 5:30 PM (specific topics not publicly disclosed)
Special order of business items considered at 6:30 PM public meeting
Two consecutive special meetings instead of regular council schedule

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — June 2, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on June 2, 2026, where they approved proclamations recognizing LGBTQ+ Pride Month in June and the UN's World Environment Day on June 5. The council also opened the floor for public comments on any city matters not listed on the formal agenda, with up to 30 minutes total for speakers.
keyTopics: LGBTQ+ Pride Month proclamation
UN World Environment Day recognition
Public comment period for non-agenda items

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 3, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara is holding Charter Review Committee meetings where residents can weigh in on potential changes to the city's governing charter. You can attend in person, join by Zoom, or call in by phone, and you can submit written comments by email.
keyTopics: Charter Review Committee meetings open to public participation
Multiple ways to join: in-person, Zoom, or phone call-in
Deadline to submit written comments: 10 a.m. one day before the meeting

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — June 2, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve a report on weed abatement assessments for 2026 and discussed new housing development rules near public transit stops. The council is working to encourage more housing construction in transit-friendly areas around bus and train stations.
keyTopics: 2026 Weed Abatement Assessment approval
New rules allowing housing development near transit stops (Tier 1 and Tier 2)
Transit-oriented housing project eligibility and location restrictions

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 27, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to review the city's five-year Capital Improvement Plan for 2027-2031 and discuss two housing and parking initiatives. The council examined a proposal for the city's Prohousing Designation Program and received updates on downtown parking modernization, though the parking discussion was postponed to a future meeting.
keyTopics: 2027-2031 Capital Improvement Plan and budget priorities
Prohousing Designation Program application review
Downtown parking modernization initiatives
Finance Committee recommendations on capital projects
